paragraph: Ho-Ho-Kus is a single-family market through and through. Over the last seven months, 29 detached homes sold at a median of $1,330,000, with a sale-to-list ratio of 1.0 and 41 percent of homes selling above asking. The median time on market was 24 days. This is a competitive but balanced market. Homes that are priced right and presented well move steadily, but sellers can't assume they'll get multiple offers or a bidding war. The price range is wide, from $785,000 to $4,950,000, which reflects the town's variety of lot sizes and home conditions rather than any real stratification by neighborhood. If you're selling, pricing competitively to your actual market value matters more than anchoring to an aspirational number. If you're buying, you'll have room to negotiate on most homes, and the best-positioned properties will still draw competition. The 24-day median tells you homes aren't sitting on the market for months, but they're also not disappearing in a week.
